ideal_must_detect.matched_text: A single contractor profile included a cluster of quasi-identifiers: date of birth 1990-07-14, stationed at our Changi spare-parts hub, role “junior treasury analyst (contract)”, device asset tag AMH-LTP-0445, internal temp ID TEMP-9841, and payroll account ending 5321
not to populate sample forms with live details

must_detect.matched_text:
1990-07-14, stationed at our Changi spare-parts hub, role “junior treasury analyst (contract)”, device asset tag AMH-LTP-0445, internal temp ID TEMP-9841, and payroll account ending 5321.
We note that the above cluster may be capable of identifying the contractor when combined with context available to HR and should be treated as personal data under the PDPA.
Apart from that cluster, remaining records were generic role descriptors (eg, “shift technician”), anonymised age bands (20s/30s), and role mailboxes such as dpo@aorakimarine.example and treasury-ops@aorakimarine.example
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation

must_not_detect.reason: Generic role descriptor not identifying an individual.
Anonymised age bands are non-identifying demographics.
Field label only; not a personal identifier value.
Explicitly stated as invalid sample value; not a real bank account.
